title: Racosperma capillare
taxonomy: Racosperma capillare is classified within the family Fabaceae. This plant is further divided into the subfamily Faboideae. At the genus level, Racosperma capillare belongs to the genus Acacia. The plant's classification also includes the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Angiospermophyta. Its classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.
uses: Racosperma capillare, also known as the prickly acacia, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its extracts have been shown to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds and skin conditions. intro: Racosperma capillare, also known as the "Queensland Silver Oak," is a small, shrub-like tree native to Australia, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers.
